title: Outer Triangle Sum
description:
You are to find the sum of the outer number of an isosceles right triangle.

For example, for n = 5 the isosceles right triangle grid are filled with non-negative integers that are less than 100:

5   
1   8 
9   6  1 
2   7  2  6 
3   5  7  8  9

The sums of the outer integers are calculated as below:

sum = 5 + 1 + 9 + 2 + 3 + 5 + 7 + 8 + 9 + 6 + 1 + 8 = 64
input: The input consists of a few test cases. For each test case, the first line of input is a positive integer n (n <= 10) that determines the dimension of the triangle. Each of the next n lines contains 1 to n integers respectively that will fill the isosceles right triangle. Input is terminated by a case where n is 0.
output: Each line of output will start with “Case #:” where # is replaced by the case number. Then you have to output the sum of the outer numbers of the triangle.
source: ICPC, Regionals, Asia Pacific, Malaysia, Malaysia National Programming Contest, Al-Khawarizmi National Programming Contest 2016, jh05013
